perturbation.original_text: The Company will use the net proceeds received in connection with the offering and sale of the Offered Shares and will cause the Operating Partnership to use the net proceeds received in connection with the issuance and sale of the Company Preferred OP Units in the manner described in the "Use of Proceeds" section of the Registration Statement, the General Disclosure Package and the Prospectus, and, except as disclosed in the Registration Statement, the General Disclosure Package and the Prospectus, the Company does not intend to use any of the proceeds from the sale of the Offered Shares hereunder to repay any outstanding debt owed to any affiliate of the Agent.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will use the net proceeds received in connection with the offering and sale of the Offered Shares and will cause the Operating Partnership to use the net proceeds received in connection with the issuance and sale of the Company Preferred OP Units in a manner determined by the Board. The specifics of the use of proceeds may be subject to change. and, except as disclosed in the Registration Statement, the General Disclosure Package and the Prospectus, the Company does not intend to use any of the proceeds from the sale of the Offered Shares hereunder to repay any outstanding debt owed to any affiliate of the Agent.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'in the manner described in the "Use of Proceeds" section of the Registration Statement, the General Disclosure Package and the Prospectus' to 'in a manner determined by the Board. The specifics of the use of proceeds may be subject to change', the contract now contradicts itself. The first statement commits to a specific usage plan detailed in the Registration Statement/Prospectus, while the revised text suggests the Board has discretion to alter this plan. This creates uncertainty because, according to section 1. (a) Representations and Warranties. The Transaction Entities, jointly and severally, represent and warrant to, and agree with, the Agent that: (i) Filing and Effectiveness of Registration Statement; Certain Defined Terms. The Company has filed with the Commission a registration statement on Form S-3 (No. 333-208956) covering the registration of the Offered Shares under the Act, including a base prospectus (the "Base Prospectus"). which it has already stated to be the case, if they can now change the way they use the profits, then all the representations and warranties done in section 1. are false. This directly creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Representations and Warranties of the Transaction Entities. (a) Representations and Warranties. The Transaction Entities, jointly and severally, represent and warrant to, and agree with, the Agent that: (i) Filing and Effectiveness of Registration Statement; Certain Defined Terms. The Company has filed with the Commission a registration statement on Form S-3 (No. 333-208956) covering the registration of the Offered Shares under the Act, including a base prospectus (the "Base Prospectus"). Such registration statement, as amended, including the financial statements, exhibits and schedules thereto, in the form in which it became effective under the Act, including all documents incorporated or deemed to be incorporated by reference therein and any information deemed to be a part thereof at the time of effectiveness pursuant to Rule 430B under the Act, shall be referred to as the "Registration Statement."
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating the 'use of proceeds may be subject to change' by the Board, directly conflicting with the original commitment to use proceeds as described in the Registration Statement, which the contradicted text establishes as a binding document. This creates uncertainty about the enforceability of representations and warranties related to the Registration Statement, thus representing a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Transaction Entities and each of their respective subsidiaries maintain (A) effective internal controls over financial reporting (as defined under Rule 13a-15 and Rule 15d-15 under the Exchange Act) and (B) a system of internal accounting controls sufficient to provide reasonable assurance that (i) transactions are executed in accordance with management's general or specific authorizations; (ii) transactions are recorded as necessary to permit preparation of financial statements in conformity with U.S. generally accepted accounting principles ("GAAP") and to maintain asset accountability; (iii) access to assets is permitted only in accordance with management's general or specific authorization; and (iv) the recorded accountability for assets is compared with the existing assets at reasonable intervals and appropriate action is taken with respect to any differences.
perturbation.changed_text: The Transaction Entities and each of their respective subsidiaries maintain a system of internal accounting controls. While we strive for accuracy, complete assurance cannot be guaranteed due to inherent limitations.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the detailed description of effective internal controls with a vague statement about maintaining 'a system of internal accounting controls' and adding a disclaimer that 'complete assurance cannot be guaranteed,' the contract introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text implies a robust system adhering to specific standards (Rule 13a-15, Rule 15d-15, GAAP), while the modified text undermines this assurance. This is an issue due to the Representation and Warranties of the Transaction Entities done on section 1. (a) Representations and Warranties. The Transaction Entities, jointly and severally, represent and warrant to, and agree with, the Agent that: (i) Filing and Effectiveness of Registration Statement; Certain Defined Terms. The Company has filed with the Commission a registration statement on Form S-3 (No. 333-208956) covering the registration of the Offered Shares under the Act, including a base prospectus (the "Base Prospectus"). which it has already stated to be the case, if they cannot guarantee effective internal controls due to inherent limitations, then all the representations and warranties done in section 1. are false. This directly creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Representations and Warranties of the Transaction Entities. (a) Representations and Warranties. The Transaction Entities, jointly and severally, represent and warrant to, and agree with, the Agent that: (i) Filing and Effectiveness of Registration Statement; Certain Defined Terms. The Company has filed with the Commission a registration statement on Form S-3 (No. 333-208956) covering the registration of the Offered Shares under the Act, including a base prospectus (the "Base Prospectus"). Such registration statement, as amended, including the financial statements, exhibits and schedules thereto, in the form in which it became effective under the Act, including all documents incorporated or deemed to be incorporated by reference therein and any information deemed to be a part thereof at the time of effectiveness pursuant to Rule 430B under the Act, shall be referred to as the "Registration Statement."
perturbation.justification: The original text asserts the maintenance of 'effective internal controls over financial reporting' adhering to specific standards. The changed text replaces this with a more general statement about maintaining a 'system of internal accounting controls' and introduces a disclaimer about the guarantee of complete assurance due to inherent limitations. This creates a contradiction because the Representations and Warranties section (specifically regarding the registration statement on Form S-3) relies on the implied existence of those effective internal controls. By admitting that complete assurance cannot be guaranteed, the changed text undermines the basis of the representations and warranties, creating a direct conflict as the prior representations are potentially false. Therefore the Changed Text contradicts the Contradicted Text as it no longer provides reasonable assurance for the representations made earlier in the document.

perturbation.original_text: The Company will use its best efforts to qualify for taxation as a REIT under the Code for its taxable year ending December 31, 2016 and thereafter, unless the Board determines that it is no longer in the best interests of the Company to continue to qualify as REIT.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company intends to qualify for taxation as a REIT, but qualification is not guaranteed. The Board retains the discretion to change the Company's structure.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'best efforts' to qualify as a REIT to a mere 'intention' and explicitly stating the Board can 'change the Company's structure', the contract creates an in-text contradiction. Previously, the clause implied a strong commitment to maintaining REIT status. This is an issue due to the Representation and Warranties of the Transaction Entities done on section 1. (a) Representations and Warranties. The Transaction Entities, jointly and severally, represent and warrant to, and agree with, the Agent that: (i) Filing and Effectiveness of Registration Statement; Certain Defined Terms. The Company has filed with the Commission a registration statement on Form S-3 (No. 333-208956) covering the registration of the Offered Shares under the Act, including a base prospectus (the "Base Prospectus"). which it has already stated to be the case, if they intend but don't qualify as REIT, then all the representations and warranties done in section 1. are false. This directly creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Representations and Warranties of the Transaction Entities. (a) Representations and Warranties. The Transaction Entities, jointly and severally, represent and warrant to, and agree with, the Agent that: (i) Filing and Effectiveness of Registration Statement; Certain Defined Terms. The Company has filed with the Commission a registration statement on Form S-3 (No. 333-208956) covering the registration of the Offered Shares under the Act, including a base prospectus (the "Base Prospectus"). Such registration statement, as amended, including the financial statements, exhibits and schedules thereto, in the form in which it became effective under the Act, including all documents incorporated or deemed to be incorporated by reference therein and any information deemed to be a part thereof at the time of effectiveness pursuant to Rule 430B under the Act, shall be referred to as the "Registration Statement."
perturbation.justification: The original text implied a strong commitment to achieving REIT status. The changed text weakens this commitment to a mere intention, while explicitly stating the Board's discretion to change the company's structure. This directly contradicts the representations and warranties in the Contradicted Text, specifically the filing and effectiveness of the registration statement based on the assumption the company is a REIT. If the company does not qualify as a REIT, the representations and warranties become false, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
